title: boesenbergia regalis
intro: Boesenbergia regalis, also known as Fingerroot, is a type of flowering plant in the ginger family (Zingiberaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Boesenbergia regalis include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as having antioxidant and antidiabetic effects. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in some traditional Chinese medicine recipes.
